useCases.title: Submission Appetite Screening Agent
statusQuo: Underwriters manually read every broker submission email and PDF ACORD form to decide if the risk is even in appetite.
A third of underwriting hours go to declining risks the carrier was never going to write.
Appetite guidelines live in a 90-page PDF that changes quarterly, so screening decisions are inconsistent across desks.
agentification: Extracts risk attributes from inbound submissions and scores them against the current appetite matrix within minutes of receipt.
Routes in-appetite submissions to the right underwriting desk and drafts decline letters for clear out-of-appetite risks.
Flags borderline risks with a rationale summary and enriches them with LexisNexis loss history before an underwriter opens the file.

useCases.title: Broker Submission Intake Orchestrator
statusQuo: Underwriting assistants rekey ACORD applications, loss runs, and schedules of values into Duck Creek by hand.
Incomplete submissions bounce back and forth with brokers over email for days before anyone notices a missing signature.
Loss runs arrive in dozens of carrier-specific formats that each require manual interpretation.
agentification: Parses ACORD forms, loss runs, and SOV spreadsheets and creates structured submission records in Duck Creek Policy automatically.
Detects missing or inconsistent fields and generates a single consolidated information request to the broker, including a DocuSign envelope for unsigned forms.
Publishes a clean submission dataset to BigQuery so intake quality and broker responsiveness can be tracked over time.

useCases.title: Small Commercial Quote-Bind STP Engine

kpis.label: Cost per bound small commercial policy

statusQuo: Small BOP and workers' comp quotes under $10K premium still queue behind large accounts for manual underwriter review.
Referral rules in PolicyCenter are so conservative that routine risks trigger the same review path as complex ones.
Brokers abandon quotes because turnaround on simple risks stretches past 48 hours.
agentification: Evaluates small commercial quotes against underwriting guidelines and historical loss data in BigQuery to clear routine risks for automatic bind.
Escalates only genuinely non-standard exposures to a human underwriter with a pre-built referral summary.
Publishes STP rate and referral-reason dashboards in Looker so the underwriting manager can tune thresholds weekly.

useCases.title: Agency Production Performance Monitor

kpis.label: Time to detect declining agency production
Agency book growth in managed territories
Distribution manager prep time per agency review

statusQuo: Agency production reports are compiled quarterly in spreadsheets, so a slipping agency isn't noticed until two quarters of premium are gone.
Distribution managers manually stitch together quote volume, hit ratio, and retention from three different systems before every agency visit.
Underperforming agencies get the same outreach cadence as top producers because no one has time to segment.
agentification: Runs weekly production scans across the Duck Creek book in BigQuery and detects agencies with falling quote volume, hit ratio, or retention.
Generates a briefing pack for each flagged agency with trend charts and recommended talking points before the distribution manager's visit.
Triggers segmented re-engagement campaigns through Salesforce Marketing Cloud for agencies that have gone quiet.

useCases.title: Renewal Risk Requalification Agent
statusQuo: Renewals are worked in expiration-date order regardless of risk change, so deteriorated risks renew as-is while good accounts get over-scrutinized.
Underwriters pull LexisNexis reports one account at a time only when something already looks wrong.
Accounts with new claims, new vehicles, or changed operations slip through renewal untouched because nobody re-verified exposures.
agentification: Requalifies every renewal 90 days out by comparing current exposures, claims activity, and LexisNexis risk signals against the expiring terms.
Recommends renew-as-is, re-rate, or non-renew treatment for each account and routes only changed risks to the underwriter queue.
Drafts renewal terms and broker talking points for accounts flagged for rate action.

useCases.title: FNOL Triage & Routing Agent
statusQuo: First notice of loss arrives through phone, portal, and email and sits in a shared queue until an intake specialist manually reads it.
Complexity and severity are judged from a quick skim, so injury claims sometimes land with adjusters licensed only for property damage.
Claimants wait a full business day just to learn who their adjuster is.
agentification: Reads every inbound FNOL, extracts loss facts, and scores severity and complexity at the moment of receipt.
Routes each claim to the best-fit adjuster based on line, severity, licensing, jurisdiction, and current workload in ClaimCenter.
Notifies the claimant immediately via Zendesk with their adjuster assignment, claim number, and next steps.

useCases.title: Subrogation Opportunity Analyzer

kpis.label: Days from payment to subro referral

statusQuo: Subrogation potential depends on the paying adjuster remembering to tick a checkbox while focused on closing the file.
Recovery opportunities in adjuster notes and police reports are never revisited once a claim closes.
Referrals arrive so late that statutes of limitation and evidence quality have already eroded recovery value.
agentification: Scans open and recently closed claims nightly, reading adjuster notes, police reports, and loss descriptions for third-party liability signals.
Scores each claim's recovery likelihood and expected value using historical recovery outcomes stored in BigQuery.
Creates prioritized subrogation referrals in ClaimCenter with the supporting evidence excerpts attached.

useCases.title: Reserve Development Early Warning Monitor
statusQuo: Reserve adequacy on individual claims is only questioned at 90-day diary reviews, long after new medical or legal facts emerged.
Managers discover under-reserved files when a settlement demand blows past the posted reserve.
Stair-stepped reserving distorts actuarial triangles and quarterly IBNR estimates.
agentification: Monitors new documents, medical bills, and attorney correspondence on every open claim for facts that change expected ultimate cost.
Compares posted reserves against model-predicted severity for similar historical claims in BigQuery and flags gaps above threshold.
Escalates under-reserved files to the claims operations manager with a suggested reserve range and the driving evidence.

useCases.title: Medical Bill Review Engine
statusQuo: Adjusters eyeball CMS-1500 and UB-04 forms line by line against fee schedules they have to look up manually.
Duplicate billings and unbundled procedure codes slip through because cross-claim comparison is impractical by hand.
Bills sit in review queues so long that prompt-pay penalties accrue in several states.
agentification: Extracts line items from incoming medical bills and validates codes against fee schedules, treatment guidelines, and the claim's injury profile.
Detects duplicates, upcoding, and unbundling by comparing against the claimant's full billing history in BigQuery.
Recommends pay, reduce, or deny per line and drafts the explanation-of-review letter for the adjuster to approve.

useCases.title: Total Loss Settlement Orchestrator

kpis.label: Rental car spend per total loss

statusQuo: Specialists manually assemble valuation reports, lien payoff letters, and title paperwork from separate portals for every totaled vehicle.
Settlement offers wait on back-and-forth phone tag with lienholders to confirm payoff amounts.
Every extra day of paperwork adds rental reimbursement expense the carrier absorbs.
agentification: Assembles the complete total loss package — valuation, lien payoff, salvage assignment, and owner-retained options — as soon as a vehicle is declared a total.
Generates settlement documents and sends them for e-signature through DocuSign with automated reminders.
Tracks title and payment milestones in ClaimCenter and escalates any file stalled more than 48 hours.

useCases.title: Premium Delinquency Outreach Agent

kpis.label: Manual dunning calls per analyst per day

statusQuo: Past-due accounts get the same three-letter dunning sequence whether the policyholder missed one installment or is chronically delinquent.
Billing analysts spend mornings dialing through a printed delinquency list with no view of which accounts are likely to self-cure.
Good customers lapse over a failed autopay card nobody told them about.
agentification: Scores every past-due account nightly on cure likelihood using payment history and policy tenure in BigQuery.
Sends personalized, channel-appropriate reminders through Salesforce Marketing Cloud, including one-tap payment links and card-update prompts.
Escalates only low-cure-probability, high-premium accounts to human outreach with a suggested payment arrangement.

useCases.title: Agent Commission Reconciliation Engine
statusQuo: Monthly commission runs are reconciled in spreadsheets that join BillingCenter output against agency agreements maintained in Word documents.
Chargebacks on cancelled and rewritten policies are applied inconsistently, generating a steady stream of agency disputes.
Overpayments are usually discovered only when an agency voluntarily reports them.
agentification: Reconciles every commission line against the governing agency agreement, policy transactions, and premium collections in BigQuery.
Flags rate mismatches, missed chargebacks, and duplicate payments before the statement run is released.
Drafts dispute-response letters with transaction-level evidence and publishes a monthly variance dashboard in Looker.

useCases.title: Cancellation Notice Compliance Agent
statusQuo: Cancellation notice timing and wording requirements differ across 50 states, and staff work from a wiki that lags regulatory changes.
A notice sent one day short of the statutory period forces the carrier to keep covering a non-paying insured.
Proof-of-mailing records are stored in three places, making defense of a disputed cancellation a scavenger hunt.
agentification: Validates every pending cancellation against the current state-specific notice period, format, and lienholder-copy requirements before release.
Generates the compliant notice, dispatches it with tracked delivery, and files proof-of-mailing evidence against the policy record.
Blocks and escalates any cancellation whose notice window or content would be defective, with the specific statute cited.

useCases.title: Payment Plan Recommendation Agent
statusQuo: Reps quote payment plan options by manually re-rating scenarios in BillingCenter while the customer waits on the line.
Struggling policyholders are offered whatever plan the rep remembers rather than the one they can actually sustain.
Fee waivers and due-date changes require a supervisor callback that half the time never happens.
agentification: Presents the rep a ranked set of viable payment plans in real time, priced from BillingCenter and fitted to the customer's payment history in BigQuery.
Recommends hardship options, due-date alignment, and fee waivers within pre-approved authority limits.
Applies the selected plan to the account and logs a complete interaction summary to Zendesk automatically.

useCases.title: Unapplied Cash Resolution Agent
statusQuo: Lockbox payments with mistyped policy numbers or agency bulk remittances land in suspense and wait for manual research.
Specialists match payments by searching BillingCenter one candidate account at a time.
Suspense balances trigger erroneous dunning notices to customers who already paid.
agentification: Matches suspense payments to open receivables using fuzzy matching across payer name, amount, bank data, and agency remittance files in BigQuery.
Applies high-confidence matches automatically and queues ambiguous ones with ranked candidates for one-click specialist review.
Suppresses dunning on accounts with pending matched payments and notifies the specialist of any item aging past 15 days.

useCases.title: Endorsement Processing Agent
statusQuo: Simple changes like adding a driver or updating a mortgagee arrive by email and queue for days behind complex work.
Reps re-read the whole policy to figure out which forms an address change actually triggers.
Typos in manually keyed endorsements generate a second round of corrections and re-issued documents.
agentification: Interprets change requests from email, portal, and call summaries and maps them to the correct endorsement transactions in PolicyCenter.
Executes routine endorsements straight through, quoting any premium change and issuing updated documents automatically.
Routes complex or underwriting-impacting changes to the right specialist with the transaction pre-staged and a summary of what changed.

useCases.title: Certificate of Insurance Issuance Agent

kpis.label: COIs with incorrect holder or coverage data

statusQuo: Certificate requests spike at contract-renewal season and pile into a shared inbox worked first-in-first-out.
Reps copy coverage limits from the policy screen into ACORD 25 templates by hand, introducing transcription errors.
Requests needing special wording or additional insured status stall while reps hunt for someone with authority to approve.
agentification: Generates ACORD certificates directly from live Duck Creek policy data the moment a request arrives, eliminating transcription.
Validates requested holder wording against approved language and auto-issues standard certificates with delivery via DocuSign.
Escalates non-standard wording or additional-insured requests to an authorized reviewer with the deviation highlighted.

useCases.title: Non-Renewal Notice Compliance Monitor

kpis.label: Forced renewals from missed notice deadlines

statusQuo: Notice deadlines ranging from 30 to 120 days by state and line are tracked in a spreadsheet updated by hand.
A missed deadline legally obligates the carrier to renew a risk underwriting wanted off the books.
Reason-for-non-renewal wording that is lawful in one state draws a market conduct inquiry in another.
agentification: Audits every planned non-renewal daily against state-specific deadline, delivery-method, and permissible-reason rules.
Alerts the servicing team weeks ahead of each notice deadline and drafts compliant notice language with the correct statutory citations.
Publishes a compliance exception report to BigQuery and escalates any at-risk deadline to the compliance officer immediately.

useCases.title: Policyholder Correspondence Drafting Agent
statusQuo: Reps compose coverage-explanation and complaint-response letters from a library of stale templates that no longer match current forms.
Every letter touching coverage interpretation waits in a supervisor review queue.
Inconsistent explanations of the same exclusion across letters create ammunition for disputes.
agentification: Drafts personalized correspondence grounded in the customer's actual Duck Creek policy forms, endorsements, and Zendesk interaction history.
Checks every draft against approved coverage-interpretation language and plain-language readability standards before it reaches review.
Routes drafts touching denials or complaints to the team lead with cited policy provisions attached for one-pass approval.

useCases.title: Mid-Term Cancellation Retention Agent

kpis.label: Time from cancel request to retention contact

statusQuo: Cancellation requests are processed transactionally with no retention attempt unless the customer happens to mention price.
By the time a weekly cancellation report reaches the retention team, most customers have already bound elsewhere.
Reps have no visibility into which discounts or coverage adjustments the departing customer actually qualifies for.
agentification: Detects cancellation intent the moment a request or competitor-comparison inquiry hits PolicyCenter and scores the account's retention value.
Generates a personalized save offer — eligible discounts, coverage rebalancing, payment plan changes — priced against the live policy.
Notifies the retention specialist within minutes for high-value accounts and triggers a tailored win-back journey in Salesforce Marketing Cloud for the rest.

useCases.title: Catastrophe Exposure Rollup Engine

kpis.label: Time to answer a reinsurer exposure query

statusQuo: Cat aggregation runs quarterly on extracts that are weeks stale by the time the rollup is finished.
New policies bound in a hurricane-exposed county between rollups are invisible to the accumulation view.
Reinsurance renewal questionnaires trigger a two-week scramble of manual geocoding and spreadsheet consolidation.
agentification: Geocodes and aggregates the full in-force book daily in BigQuery, enriched with Verisk hazard and construction data.
Monitors accumulation against zone appetite limits and escalates any county or coastal band approaching capacity before new business breaches it.
Publishes live PML and TIV dashboards in Looker and generates reinsurer-ready exposure schedules on demand.

useCases.title: Reserve Adequacy Analyzer
statusQuo: Actuaries spend the first two weeks of every quarter assembling and cleaning loss triangles before analysis can even start.
Development anomalies hide inside aggregated triangles until they are large enough to move the whole segment.
Case reserve philosophy shifts in claims go unnoticed by actuarial until they distort paid-to-incurred ratios.
agentification: Builds clean, reconciled loss triangles from ClaimCenter transactional data in BigQuery continuously rather than quarterly.
Runs multiple development methods per segment, detects diagnostics drifting outside expected ranges, and flags case-reserving pattern shifts.
Drafts the quarterly reserve review deck with segment-level commentary and highlights the exhibits that most need actuarial judgment.

useCases.title: Loss Ratio Trend Monitor

kpis.label: Time to detect a deteriorating segment
Combined ratio impact of late detection

statusQuo: Loss ratio reviews happen monthly at line-of-business level, hiding a deteriorating state-class-tier cell inside a healthy aggregate.
Analysts manually cut the data a few dimensions at a time and inevitably miss interaction effects.
By the time deterioration is confirmed, two more months of underpriced business has been written.
agentification: Continuously scans loss ratios across hundreds of state, class, tier, and cohort combinations in BigQuery, benchmarked against Verisk industry trends.
Distinguishes credible deterioration from noise using severity, frequency, and large-loss decomposition.
Escalates confirmed adverse trends to the chief actuary with a driver analysis and recommends which cells to fast-track for rate review.

useCases.title: Rate Indication Preparation Engine

kpis.label: Filings completed per actuary per year

statusQuo: Each state rate indication is a bespoke spreadsheet effort: pulling premium and loss data, trending, developing, and on-leveling by hand.
Actuaries re-derive the same trend and development selections for every filing because prior work isn't systematically reusable.
Filing exhibits get inconsistent formatting across states, inviting avoidable regulator objections.
agentification: Assembles the full indication data set — earned premium at current rate level, developed and trended losses, expense provisions — from BigQuery on request.
Applies Verisk loss cost trends and prior filing selections as starting points, showing the actuary exactly what changed since the last review.
Generates regulator-formatted filing exhibits and drafts responses to standard objection letters from the filing's own support.

useCases.title: Premium Leakage Detection Analyzer

kpis.label: Audit hit rate (audits finding material misstatement)

statusQuo: Premium audits are scheduled by policy size and rotation, not by likelihood of misreported payroll, mileage, or class codes.
Auditors discover undisclosed business operations only if the insured volunteers them during a site visit.
Unrated exposure — extra drivers, new locations, higher payrolls — accrues all term and is often never recovered.
agentification: Cross-references declared exposures against LexisNexis business intelligence, public filings, and telematics-derived signals in BigQuery.
Scores every policy for leakage probability and expected recovery, and recommends which audits to prioritize each month.
Drafts pre-audit findings summaries for auditors and publishes recovered-premium tracking in Looker.

useCases.title: SIU Referral Scoring Engine
statusQuo: Referrals depend on adjuster intuition, so the SIU queue mixes solid cases with hunches that consume investigator weeks.
Fraud indicators buried in claim notes and documents are never systematically compared across the book.
Most fraud is detected after indemnity payments have already gone out the door.
agentification: Rescored every open claim on each new document or transaction, combining FRISS indicators with text signals mined from ClaimCenter notes.
Routes high-score claims to SIU with an evidence dossier summarizing the specific red flags and their sources.
Recommends payment holds on claims crossing the fraud threshold and notifies the assigned adjuster with the rationale.

useCases.title: Fraud Ring Network Analyzer

kpis.label: Organized fraud rings identified per year

statusQuo: Investigators spot ring activity only when the same body shop or clinic name happens to catch someone's eye across files.
Shared phone numbers, addresses, and bank accounts across claims are invisible without manual cross-referencing.
Staged-accident rings rotate participants faster than case-by-case investigation can connect them.
agentification: Builds entity-resolution graphs across claims, providers, and participants nightly in BigQuery, enriched with LexisNexis identity data.
Detects suspicious clusters — shared identifiers, provider billing patterns, choreographed loss sequences — and scores them for organized activity.
Creates ring case files with network visualizations and recommends which claims to consolidate under a single SIU investigation.

useCases.title: Application Fraud Screening Agent

kpis.label: False-positive hold rate on clean applications

statusQuo: Garaging address fraud, undisclosed drivers, and prior-carrier cancellation history surface only after a claim is filed.
Analysts manually spot-check a small sample of new business applications against third-party data.
Agent-assisted rate manipulation patterns go undetected because no one aggregates application anomalies by producer.
agentification: Screens every application at quote and bind against FRISS device, identity, and behavior signals plus LexisNexis household and vehicle data.
Flags discrepancies — garaging mismatches, undisclosed operators, recycled identities — and routes them for verification before issue.
Aggregates anomaly patterns by producer in BigQuery and escalates suspicious agency-level clusters to the fraud analyst.

useCases.title: Regulatory Complaint Response Agent
statusQuo: Each DOI complaint kicks off a manual hunt across policy, claims, billing, and correspondence systems to reconstruct what happened.
Response drafting starts from scratch every time, even for recurring complaint themes.
Deadlines vary by state and are tracked in a shared calendar that has missed entries before.
agentification: Assembles the complete customer file — policy transactions from PolicyCenter, correspondence, Zendesk interactions — the moment a DOI complaint is logged.
Drafts the regulator response with a factual timeline and citations to the governing policy provisions and state rules.
Tracks every statutory deadline, escalates responses at risk of lateness, and publishes complaint-trend analytics to BigQuery for root-cause review.

useCases.title: Market Conduct Exam Prep Orchestrator
statusQuo: A market conduct data call pulls a dozen staff off their jobs for weeks to extract, reconcile, and format policy and claims records.
Self-audit happens only after an exam is announced, so known issues surface with no time to remediate.
Prior exam findings and remediation commitments live in scattered documents nobody re-checks until the next exam.
agentification: Maintains exam-ready data marts in BigQuery mapped to NAIC market conduct standards, refreshed from PolicyCenter continuously.
Runs ongoing self-audits against timeliness, disclosure, and handling standards and escalates emerging violations before regulators find them.
Generates examiner data-call packages in the requested format and drafts responses to prior-finding follow-ups with remediation evidence attached.
